scope.summary: Rust block_on reporting now consumes nominal tokio runtime parameter evidence for Runtime and Handle receivers.

capability_alignment.capability: receiver type provenance for future drive scheduling attribution
not_a_feature_list: No exact admission path was opened for Rust block_on or async/sync equivalence.
No new public semantic-pack API was added.
The verifier reuses DomainEvidence::Nominal instead of adding a block_on-specific API.
Struct-field receivers, type aliases, nested brace imports, and unknown local receivers remain closed.
reused_capabilities: shared future-drive scheduling and future-settled value-channel obligations

new_reporting_surfaces.surface: use tokio::runtime::Runtime; fn run(rt: Runtime) { rt.block_on(...) }
new_reporting_surfaces.guard: The parameter type must be backed by exact tokio::runtime::Runtime import evidence from the same module scope, and the receiver name must still refer to that parameter.
Reference parameter types preserve the same nominal runtime identity proof.

new_reporting_surfaces.surface: use tokio::runtime::Handle as TokioHandle; fn run(handle: TokioHandle) { handle.block_on(...) }
new_reporting_surfaces.guard: Aliases are accepted only when the local type name has exact imported-binding evidence for tokio::runtime::Handle from the same module scope.
Fully qualified tokio runtime parameter types are accepted only when the file does not define a project-local tokio root.

assertions: Imported tokio Runtime parameter domain evidence depends on the imported binding symbol evidence.
Scoped tokio Runtime parameter domain evidence depends on imports from the parameter module scope.
Aliased tokio Handle parameter domain evidence depends on the aliased imported binding symbol evidence.
Qualified tokio Runtime parameter evidence is accepted without an import dependency.
Project-local tokio modules and same-named non-tokio Runtime imports do not record tokio nominal parameter evidence.
Runtime, &Runtime, aliased Handle, and fully qualified Runtime parameter receivers report future-drive and future-settled obligations.
A local Runtime type stays closed.
A non-tokio Runtime import stays closed.
A project-local tokio module stays closed.
A case-mismatched Tokio module path stays closed.
A parent-module import does not prove a child-module Runtime parameter.
A parameter receiver shadowed by a local binding before block_on stays closed.

current_crates_gate.note: The local crates surface has no proof-backed Rust parameter block_on bridge rows.

summary.primary_reason_note: The matching block_on units also carry async-await evidence, which remains the primary scheduling subreason by rule order.
The matching block_on unit also carries async-await and async-block evidence, which remain the primary scheduling subreason by rule order.

summary.closed_surface: Runtime parameter type imported through nested brace use tokio::{ runtime::{Builder, Runtime}, ... }.
self.rt.block_on(...) field receiver even though the struct field is typed tokio::runtime::Handle.

closed_surfaces.surface: self.rt.block_on(...) or other field-backed runtime receivers
closed_surfaces.reason: The reporter does not infer struct-field tokio runtime types in this slice.

closed_surfaces.surface: use tokio::{ runtime::{Runtime, Handle}, ... } short-name parameters
closed_surfaces.reason: The current Rust import evidence parser does not model nested brace imports.

closed_surfaces.surface: type Rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime; fn run(rt: Rt) { rt.block_on(...) }
closed_surfaces.reason: Rust type aliases are not converted into nominal receiver domain evidence in this slice.

closed_surfaces.surface: Runtime parameters from project-local tokio roots or non-tokio modules
closed_surfaces.reason: Same-named local or third-party types do not prove tokio runtime receiver semantics.

closed_surfaces.surface: child-module Runtime parameters with only a parent-module use tokio::runtime::Runtime import
closed_surfaces.reason: Short-name parameter evidence requires a same-module-scope imported binding.

closed_surfaces.surface: Exact convergence between block_on, await, and synchronous payload values
closed_surfaces.reason: Future drive scheduling, wake/poll behavior, cancellation/liveness, result-channel, and receiver/type identity contracts remain missing evidence.

next_followups: Add nested-brace Rust import evidence so tokio::{ runtime::{Runtime, Handle}, ... } can feed the same parameter receiver capability.
Add struct-field type provenance for self.rt.block_on only after field declarations, constructor assignment, and receiver aliasing can be proven fail-closed.
Consider post-lower Rust parameter-domain recording if import order independence needs to be modeled for short-name parameter types.
